# The SMS & RCS Field Guide

> A pattern library of 75 SMS and 120 RCS campaign use cases across 15 APAC verticals, built on CleverTap, plus a Notify SMS transactional track covering the promotional versus transactional split. Each pattern gives the message, the trigger that makes it land, per-market compliance and RCS availability, and how to build it in CleverTap. It also includes a plain SMS vs WhatsApp vs RCS comparison. This is a directional reference, not a template dump; verify sender-ID registration, consent and carrier availability before any send.

## What this is
- 75 SMS patterns across 12 verticals, grouped by SMS job (reachability floor, no-app reach, transactional cross-sell, renewal/expiry, time-critical transactional, compliance-grade alert, mass ops reach) and message pattern (one-off, recurring, automated/triggered, automated/recurring, journey/omni).
- 120 RCS patterns across 15 verticals (8 per vertical), grouped by RCS template type and job.
- Colour marks the vertical; a badge marks the job or template; traffic-light flags mark delivery readiness.

## SMS vs WhatsApp vs RCS
- SMS: reaches any phone with no app and no internet; text plus a link only; ~160 characters; no branding. Best for reachability floor, OTP, time-critical alerts and no-app reach.
- WhatsApp: reaches users with the app plus opt-in; rich media, buttons, lists and flows; needs Meta template approval and session pricing. Best for rich two-way in WhatsApp-heavy markets.
- RCS: reaches the native inbox with no app where the device and carrier support it; cards, carousels, up to four buttons, a verified brand and read receipts; falls back to SMS when unavailable. Best for branded, richer-than-SMS reach with a safety net.

## RCS template types (CleverTap, via Infobip)
- Text: up to 1000 characters, formatting, up to 4 buttons.
- Rich Card: media plus title (200 chars) plus description (2000 chars) plus up to 4 buttons; vertical 3:1 or 2:1, or horizontal 3:4.
- Carousel: up to 10 swipeable Rich Cards; vertical 4:5 or 5:4, or horizontal 2:1 or 4:3.
- Buttons: URL Redirect (static, dynamic, or CleverTap click-tracking), Dialer, Quick Reply. Max 4; rendering varies by device.
- Sends like any CleverTap campaign: targeting, scheduling, personalisation and SMS fallback. India needs pre-approved templates (status within ~24h). RCS Business Messaging is not end-to-end encrypted, so keep OTPs and high-sensitivity content on an encrypted channel.

## RCS availability across APAC (2026)
- Live for business messaging: India, Indonesia, Singapore, Japan, South Korea (also UAE and Saudi Arabia).
- Not yet or partial (default to WhatsApp or SMS): Philippines, Australia, New Zealand, Thailand, Vietnam, Malaysia (partial).
- iPhone supports RCS from iOS 18 and RCS for Business from iOS 18.1, limited to supporting carriers. Re-check carrier status quarterly.

## Real CleverTap reference
- Bandhan AMC (India, Investment & Banking) used RCS rich cards, carousels and one-to-one MP4 video and reported an 8-10% conversion uplift, 16,000 personalised videos, and a 75% cost reduction versus legacy and WhatsApp messaging. Source: https://clevertap.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Bandhan-AMC-Caselet.pdf

## Notify SMS (transactional track)
- Notify SMS is presented as a comparison rather than a use case gallery, because transactional SMS differs mechanically and legally rather than visually.
- Status: Notify SMS does not appear in CleverTap's public documentation at the time of writing. Every CleverTap-specific fact in that section is marked "not stated in the documentation" and listed as an open question, rather than inferred.
- What is documented today: transactional sending is configured through the provider-level SMS Type setting, with the values transactional, transactional_opt_in and promotional. Using more than one type requires a separate provider configuration for each. Routes differ at the provider (for MSG91, route=1 is promotional and route=4 is transactional).
- Classification test: a message is transactional when the user's own action or obligation caused it, it would still need sending if they had never opted into marketing, and it contains no offer, upsell, discount or cross-sell. Any promotional line reclassifies the whole message as promotional.
- India: TRAI requires entity, sender ID and template registration on the DLT portal for both promotional and transactional senders. URLs in marketing SMS must be pre-approved on DLT.
- Singapore: marketing messages need a DNC Registry check no more than 30 days before sending; transactional messages such as order confirmations, appointment reminders and account notifications generally sit outside the DNC obligation. Sender IDs must be registered with the SMS Sender ID Registry.
- Vietnam: Decree 91/2020 requires prior express consent for advertising, a national DoNotCall list, per-day limits, a permitted sending window and an advertising label. A pre-registered transaction route exists for notification and OTP traffic.
- South Korea: marketing messages carry an advertising label and unsubscribe details, consent is reconfirmed every two years, and alphanumeric sender IDs are not supported.
- Misuse boundary: a transactional route is not a way around consent, quiet hours, or opt-out, and it is not a cheaper promotional channel.
